description: Viewing Conditions: full-sun, at feeder, distance ~ 20 ft through 8 and 10 power binocs for 5-10 minutes and then 20 minutes when it returned.  First Impression: What just flew by my window with the distinct white wing patches?  Size: in yard with HOFIs and AMROs, size distinctly in between.  Bill: Distinctly grosbeak-large. Not bi-colored. Seen in excellent light in full sun at feeder for ~ 25-30 minutes. Looked at bill color as bird turned both profiles into view. Also seen briefly through a window at about 3 feet.  Breast: Pale pink just below neck.  Underwing coverts: distinctly light pink, no yellowish tinge. Saw the bird depart for flight through the bincoculars several times-only pink.   Head: Broad white stripe just above/at eye.Behaviors: Eating black oil sunflower and safflower seeds from two feeders. In company of other seed eaters (HOFIs).   In flight: Conspicuous white wing patches drew immediate attention.Call: not heardPlumage: 1st winter male
